87	/-- Elementwise fibre-side `appLE` coherence: restricting the image of `appLE`
88	equals `appLE` into the smaller open. -/
89	private lemma appLE_res_apply {S X : Scheme.{u}} (p : X ⟶ S) {U : S.Opens}
90	    {W W' : X.Opens} (e : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U) (h : W' ≤ W) (u : Γ(S, U)) :
91	    (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE h).op).hom ((p.appLE U W e).hom u) =
92	      (p.appLE U W' (h.trans e)).hom u := by
93	  have h1 := congrArg (fun (φ : Γ(S, U) ⟶ Γ(X, W')) => φ.hom u)
94	    (Scheme.Hom.appLE_map (f := p) e (homOfLE h).op)
95	  simpa only [CommRingCat.hom_comp, RingHom.comp_apply] using h1
96

98	equals `appLE` from the larger base open. -/

101	    (p.appLE U' W e').hom ((S.presheaf.map (homOfLE h).op).hom u) =
102	      (p.appLE U W e).hom u := by
103	  have h1 := congrArg (fun (φ : Γ(S, U) ⟶ Γ(X, W)) => φ.hom u)
104	    (Scheme.Hom.map_appLE (f := p) e' (homOfLE h).op)
105	  simpa only [CommRingCat.hom_comp, RingHom.comp_apply] using h1

121	/-- **Mixed-base stability of flatness under module localization.**  Let `B` be an
122	`R`-algebra, `N` a `B`-module that is flat over `R`, and `g : N →ₗ[B] N'` a
123	localization of `N` at a submonoid `p ⊆ B`.  Then `N'` is still flat over `R`.
124	
125	Mathlib's `Module.Flat.of_isLocalizedModule` treats only submonoids of `R`
126	itself; here the localization happens on the fibre side.  The proof rewrites
127	`𝟙 N' ⊗ f` as the `IsLocalizedModule.map` of `𝟙 N ⊗ f` (Mathlib's
128	`IsLocalizedModule.map_lTensor`, with the `IsLocalizedModule.rTensor` instances)
129	and uses that localization preserves injectivity
130	(`IsLocalizedModule.map_injective`). -/
131	theorem _root_.Module.Flat.of_isLocalizedModule_algebra {R B : Type v} [CommRing R]
132	    [CommRing B] [Algebra R B] (p : Submonoid B) {N N' : Type v} [AddCommGroup N]
133	    [Module R N] [Module B N] [IsScalarTower R B N] [AddCommGroup N'] [Module R N']
134	    [Module B N'] [IsScalarTower R B N'] (g : N →ₗ[B] N') [IsLocalizedModule p g]
135	    [Module.Flat R N] : Module.Flat R N' := by
136	  rw [Module.Flat.iff_lTensor_injectiveₛ]
137	  intro P _ _ I
138	  rw [← TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.coe_lTensor (A := B)]
139	  rw [← IsLocalizedModule.map_lTensor (S := p) (g := g) (f := I.subtype)]
140	  exact IsLocalizedModule.map_injective p
141	    (TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.rTensor R I g)
142	    (TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.rTensor R P g)
143	    (TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.lTensor B N I.subtype)
144	    (by
145	      rw [TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.coe_lTensor (A := B)]
146	      exact Module.Flat.lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap I.subtype
147	        Subtype.val_injective)

261	/-- **Two-layer basic-open reduction for generic flatness.**  Given affine charts
262	`Wc j ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀` covering `p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀`, all of whose section modules localized
263	at `f ∈ Γ(S, U₀)` are free (the output of algebraic generic freeness at a
264	common denominator `f`), every affine pair `U ≤ D(f)`, `W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U` has flat
265	sections: cover `W` by opens simultaneously basic in `W` and in a chart, lying
266	above base basic opens common to `U` and `U₀`; conclude with
267	`Module.flat_of_isLocalized_span` over `Γ(X, W)` and the two
268	`Module.flat_iff_of_isLocalization` exchanges on the base. -/
269	private theorem flat_section_pair {S X : Scheme.{u}} (p : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules)
270	    [F.IsQuasicoherent] {U₀ : S.Opens} (hU₀ : IsAffineOpen U₀)
271	    {ι : Type u} (Wc : ι → X.Opens) (hWc : ∀ j, IsAffineOpen (Wc j))
272	    (eWc : ∀ j, Wc j ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀)
273	    (hcover : ∀ x ∈ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀, ∃ j, x ∈ Wc j) (f : Γ(S, U₀))
274	    (hfree : ∀ j,
275	      letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, Wc j) :=
276	        Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ (Wc j) (eWc j)).hom
277	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f)
278	        (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) Γ(F, Wc j)))
279	    {U : S.Opens} (hU : IsAffineOpen U) (hUf : U ≤ S.basicOpen f)

283	  letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, W) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U W e).hom
284	  have hUU₀ : U ≤ U₀ := hUf.trans (S.basicOpen_le f)
285	  have hWU₀ : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀ := e.trans (fun x hx => hUU₀ hx)

287	  have Hx : ∀ x : ↥W, ∃ (b : Γ(X, W)) (a : Γ(S, U)) (a' : Γ(S, U₀)) (j : ι)
288	      (c : Γ(X, Wc j)),
289	      X.basicOpen b = X.basicOpen c ∧ (x : X) ∈ X.basicOpen b ∧

291	      X.basicOpen b ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ (S.basicOpen a) := by

294	    have hxU₀ : p.base x.1 ∈ U₀ := hUU₀ hxU

299	    have hxT : x.1 ∈ (W ⊓ (p ⁻¹ᵁ S.basicOpen a ⊓ Wc j) : X.Opens) :=

721	affine: it is isomorphic to the pullback of the restricted affine cospan
722	(`Scheme.Hom.isPullback_resLE`). -/
723	theorem isAffineOpen_pullback_piece (hUS : IsAffineOpen US) (hUT : IsAffineOpen UT)
724	    (hUX : IsAffineOpen UX) : IsAffineOpen (g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) := by
725	  have : IsAffine US := hUS
726	  have : IsAffine UT := hUT
727	  have : IsAffine UX := hUX
728	  exact IsAffine.of_isIso (Scheme.Hom.isPullback_resLE H hUST hUSX rfl).isoPullback.hom

730	/-- The section-ring square of a fibre-product square at an affine piece is a
731	pushout in `CommRingCat` (Mathlib's `isIso_pushoutSection_of_isAffineOpen`,
732	repackaged). -/
733	theorem isPushout_appLE_pullback_piece (hUS : IsAffineOpen US) (hUT : IsAffineOpen UT)
734	    (hUX : IsAffineOpen UX) :
735	    CategoryTheory.IsPushout (iX.appLE US UX hUSX) (f.appLE US UT hUST)
736	      (g.appLE UX (g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) inf_le_left)
737	      (iY.appLE UT (g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) inf_le_right) :=
738	  (isIso_pushoutSection_iff H hUST hUSX rfl).mp
739	    (isIso_pushoutSection_of_isAffineOpen H hUST hUSX rfl hUS hUT hUX)

1996	/-- **Universal property of the flat-locus stratification (the `n = 0`
1997	flattening stratification)** [Nitsure §4, special case, parts (i) + (ii)].
1998	
1999	For `S` noetherian and `𝓕` a coherent `𝓞_S`-module there is a *finite*
2000	locally-closed stratification `{S_f}` of `S` — immersions, set-theoretically
2001	covering `|S|`, pairwise disjoint, `𝓕|_{S_f}` flat over `S_f` — such that,
2002	writing `i : ∐ S_f ⟶ S` for `Sigma.desc` of the inclusions, every morphism
2003	`φ : T ⟶ S` for which `φ^*𝓕` is flat over `T` factors *uniquely* through
2004	`i`.
2005	
2006	Source: [Nitsure], §4, proof of the flattening-stratification theorem,
2007	special case `n = 0` (Nakayama prolongation of a fibre basis to a local
2008	presentation `𝓞_V^{⊕m} →ψ 𝓞_V^{⊕e} → 𝓕|_V → 0`, the closed subscheme
2009	`V_e ⊆ V` cut out by the entry ideal of `ψ`, base change: `f^*𝓕` is locally
2010	free of rank `e` iff `f^*ψ = 0` iff `f` factors through `V_e`; the local
2011	strata glue by their universal property).  Flatness replaces
2012	locally-free-of-rank-`e`: for finitely presented `𝓕` they agree, with the
2013	rank decomposing `T` into clopen pieces.
